agent_output:
```json
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering expedited courier service with a target 2-hour delivery window, premium pricing, real-time tracking, and a conditional refund policy for missed windows. Synthesized from raw product discovery workshop notes involving PM, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Legal.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery Fulfillment",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery option targeting a 2-hour delivery window from order placement to delivery completion, available to customers within 10km of a warehouse." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "A $12.99 surcharge applied on top of the base delivery fee when a customer selects Express Delivery." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery fee charged for all deliveries regardless of express status. Never refunded under the express SLA." },
      { "term": "Delivery Window", "definition": "The 2-hour target timeframe within which an express delivery should be completed, starting from order placement." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The 15-minute internal operational target for a driver to pick up an express order from the warehouse after order placement." },
      { "term": "Eligible Driver", "definition": "A driver with a rating of 4.0 stars or above who is qualified to fulfill express deliveries." },
      { "term": "Warehouse Radius", "definition": "The 10km maximum distance between a customer's delivery address and the nearest warehouse, required for express eligibility." },
      { "term": "Missed Window", "definition": "An express delivery that is not completed within the 2-hour target from order placement." },
      { "term": "Goodwill Refund", "definition": "A refund of the express fee (not the base delivery fee) issued as a goodwill gesture — not a contractual obligation — when the delivery window is missed and no disqualifying conditions apply." },
      { "term": "Driver Cancellation", "definition": "When an assigned express driver abandons or cancels the delivery mid-fulfillment, triggering the replacement assignment process." },
      { "term": "Replacement Window", "definition": "The 10-minute target to assign a new eligible driver after a driver cancellation on an express order." },
      { "term": "Wrong Address Disqualification", "definition": "A condition where the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, disqualifying them from receiving a goodwill refund for a missed window." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "A customer may only select Express Delivery if their delivery address is within 10km of an active warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Physical constraint — express fulfillment is impossible if inventory cannot reach the customer within the delivery window. Allowing orders outside this radius would guarantee failure.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any order with express selected MUST have a delivery address within 10km of at least one warehouse; orders outside this radius must be rejected at checkout."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Express orders must be picked up from the warehouse within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational target that enables the 2-hour window but could be adjusted as logistics improve (e.g., faster warehouse processing, closer micro-fulfillment centers).",
      "testable_assertion": "If an express order has not been picked up within 15 minutes, the system must escalate to operations (exact escalation path TBD)."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above are eligible to be assigned express deliveries.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Quality threshold policy that operations leadership could raise or lower based on driver supply and performance data.",
      "testable_assertion": "The system MUST NOT assign an express delivery to a driver with a rating below 4.0 stars."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "The express fee is $12.99, charged on top of the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing is a business policy subject to market conditions, promotions, and A/B testing.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a customer selects Express Delivery, exactly $12.99 is added to the order total in addition to the base delivery fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When a delivery window is missed and the customer is not disqualified, only the express fee is refunded — never the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "The base delivery still occurred; the service (moving the package) was rendered. Refunding it would mean the company pays for a completed delivery. This is a financial integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "A goodwill refund for a missed express window MUST equal exactly the express fee amount and MUST NOT include the base delivery fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No goodwill refund is issued if the customer provided a wrong delivery address that contributed to the missed window.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Policy decision about fault attribution. Could be softened for high-value customers or tightened with stricter address validation.",
      "testable_assertion": "If a missed-window express delivery is flagged as 'wrong address provided by customer,' the system MUST NOT auto-issue a goodwill refund."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "If an express driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement eligible driver must be assigned within 10 minutes or a goodwill refund is automatically triggered.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 10-minute threshold and automatic refund are customer success policies that could be tuned based on operational capacity and customer satisfaction metrics.",
      "testable_assertion": "When an express driver cancels: (a) a replacement driver search begins immediately, (b) if no eligible driver is assigned within 10 minutes, the system auto-initiates a goodwill refund of the express fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "Express delivery customers must have access to real-time driver tracking from pickup through delivery.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "UX feature requirement; the delivery itself still functions without tracking. Could be degraded gracefully if GPS is unavailable.",
      "testable_assertion": "From the moment an express order is picked up until delivery completion, the customer-facing app must display the driver's live location updated at least every 30 seconds (update frequency TBD)."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from the warehouse boundary", "expected_behavior": "Needs a clear rule: is 10km inclusive or exclusive? Recommend inclusive (<=10km) to avoid customer confusion at the boundary." },
    { "scenario": "Driver rating drops below 4.0 stars mid-delivery", "expected_behavior": "The delivery should be completed by the current driver — the rating threshold applies at assignment time, not mid-fulfillment. Reassigning mid-delivery would harm the customer." },
    { "scenario": "No eligible drivers available at order time", "expected_behavior": "Express option should be hidden or disabled at checkout. If no driver can pick up within 15 minutes, the system should not accept an express order." },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels and replacement also cancels", "expected_behavior": "Cascading cancellation — does the 10-minute replacement window reset? Recommend: refund immediately on second cancellation regardless of time." },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address after order placement to one outside 10km radius", "expected_behavior": "Should this be blocked? If allowed, does it void the express SLA? Needs a clear policy." },
    { "scenario": "Warehouse runs out of stock after express order is placed", "expected_behavior": "Not addressed. Needs cancellation policy and whether full refund (express + base) or just express fee is returned." },
    { "scenario": "Delivery completed at 2h00m01s — one second over the window", "expected_behavior": "Needs a tolerance/grace period policy. Recommend: delivery completion timestamp is authoritative; no grace period but use server-side time, not driver-reported time." },
    { "scenario": "Wrong address is partially wrong (e.g., correct street, wrong apartment number)", "expected_behavior": "Who determines 'wrong address'? Needs a dispute resolution process and clear definition of what constitutes a 'wrong address' for refund disqualification." },
    { "scenario": "Refund auto-triggered by BR-7 but customer also had wrong address", "expected_behavior": "Conflicting disqualification: BR-7 says auto-refund on replacement timeout, BR-6 says no refund on wrong address. Which takes precedence? Needs explicit priority." },
    { "scenario": "Multiple warehouses within 10km — which one fulfills?", "expected_behavior": "Not specified. Needs routing logic — nearest warehouse, warehouse with inventory, or warehouse with available drivers?" }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee ($12.99) represents a new revenue stream. Goodwill refund liability is uncapped — if operational SLAs are frequently missed, refund costs could erode or exceed express revenue. The 3-business-day refund processing window helps cash flow but may frustrate customers. No mention of refund caps or abuse prevention.",
    "operational": "Constraining express to 4-star+ drivers reduces the eligible driver pool, which may cause supply crunches during peak hours. The 15-minute pickup SLA requires warehouse operations to prioritize express orders, potentially delaying standard orders. The 10-minute replacement driver assignment creates a hard operational pressure that needs dispatcher tooling or automation.",
    "user": "Real-time tracking and a prominent 2-hour window set high customer expectations. The gap between marketing language ('guaranteed') and legal reality ('best effort') is a trust risk. Automatic refunds on driver cancellation are customer-friendly but the wrong-address disqualification needs careful UX to avoid disputes.",
    "risk": "The PM-Legal contradiction on 'guarantee' vs 'best effort' is the highest risk item — marketing this as a guarantee while Legal disclaims it could lead to regulatory action or class-action exposure in jurisdictions with strict consumer protection laws. Additionally, the lack of a clear dispute process for wrong-address determinations creates customer service escalation risk.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "New premium option with high expectations. Risk of dissatisfaction if 'guarantee' language is used but refunds are denied or delayed." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "Only 4-star+ drivers qualify, creating a two-tier system. Express assignments may carry more pressure (15-min pickup). Need to understand compensation — is there an express driver premium?" },
      { "group": "Warehouse Operations", "impact": "Must prioritize express orders for sub-15-minute pick-and-pack. May need process changes, dedicated express lanes, or staffing adjustments." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Will handle disputes around wrong-address refund denials, near-miss delivery windows, and the gap between 'guaranteed' marketing and 'best effort' legal reality." },
      { "group": "Finance/Accounting", "impact": "Must process goodwill refunds within 3 business days, track express fee revenue vs refund liability, and handle the accounting distinction between contractual refunds and goodwill gestures." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must resolve the guarantee vs best-effort language before launch. Needs to review all customer-facing copy and terms of service." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 38,
    "reasoning": "The spec contains a fundamental unresolved contradiction between PM ('guaranteed') and Legal ('best effort / not a guarantee'). This is not a minor wording issue — it changes the entire product's obligation model, refund policy enforceability, and customer-facing language. Until PM and Legal align, acceptance criteria cannot be finalized because we don't know what we're promising. Additionally, several operational details are missing (escalation paths, dispute resolution, driver compensation, stock-out handling).",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "10km warehouse radius as an eligibility gate",
      "Express fee is a surcharge on top of base delivery fee",
      "Only the express fee is refundable, never the base fee",
      "4-star minimum driver rating for express eligibility",
      "Real-time tracking is a required capability"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour window is a contractual guarantee or best-effort target — this changes everything downstream",
      "Whether refunds are automatic entitlements or discretionary goodwill gestures",
      "Dispute resolution process for wrong-address determinations",
      "Driver compensation model for express deliveries",
      "Escalation path when pickup SLA is missed",
      "Priority rules when BR-6 (wrong address) and BR-7 (replacement timeout) conflict"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 2-hour window starts at order placement time, not at pickup time — PM said 'within 2 hours' and Operations referenced pickup as a sub-step",
      "The 10km radius is calculated as straight-line distance, not driving distance — needs clarification",
      "Driver rating is the platform's existing rating system, not a separate express-specific rating",
      "A single order cannot be split across multiple warehouses",
      "'Wrong address' means the address provided by the customer at checkout, not a subsequent change"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "PM states the 2-hour delivery is 'guaranteed' and missed windows trigger a refund. Legal explicitly states this is 'best effort, not a contractual guarantee' and the refund is a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' These are mutually exclusive product definitions — a guarantee with automatic refunds vs. a best-effort target with discretionary goodwill. This affects every customer-facing touchpoint, the refund policy architecture, and regulatory risk.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Legal/Compliance", "Customer Support", "Marketing", "Finance/Accounting"],
      "options": [
        "A: Contractual guarantee with automatic refunds (PM's version) — higher customer trust, higher liability, potential regulatory risk if fulfillment rates are low",
        "B: Best-effort SLA with discretionary goodwill refunds (Legal's version) — lower liability, but marketing must avoid 'guarantee' language, refund logic needs manual review gates",
        "C: Hybrid — market as 'Express Target' without guarantee language, but auto-refund as policy (not obligation) with the ability to deny in specific cases (wrong address, force majeure)"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — it satisfies Legal's requirement to avoid contractual guarantees while preserving the customer-friendly auto-refund behavior PM wants. However, PM and Legal MUST align on exact customer-facing language before any implementation begins. Recommend a joint session."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "When BR-6 (no refund for wrong address) and BR-7 (auto-refund if replacement not assigned in 10 minutes) both apply to the same order — e.g., driver cancels mid-delivery AND the address was wrong — which rule takes precedence? No stakeholder addressed this conflict.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customer Support", "Customers", "Finance/Accounting"],
      "options": [
        "A: Wrong-address disqualification always wins — no refund regardless of driver cancellation",
        "B: Auto-refund on replacement timeout always wins — customer gets refund even with wrong address",
        "C: Case-by-case manual review when both conditions are present"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — if the customer caused the problem with a wrong address, the operational failure (driver cancellation/replacement timeout) is a downstream consequence. But this needs explicit sign-off from Customer Success."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No stakeholder addressed: (1) driver compensation for express deliveries — are drivers paid more? This affects driver willingness and supply, (2) what happens when express orders are placed but no eligible drivers are available, (3) stock-out scenarios after order placement, (4) whether 10km is straight-line or driving distance, (5) the definition and adjudication process for 'wrong address.'",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Drivers", "Operations", "Customers", "Customer Support"],
      "options": [
        "Conduct a follow-up discovery session with Operations and Driver Experience teams to close these gaps before implementation"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Block implementation until at minimum the guarantee-vs-best-effort contradiction is resolved and driver availability / wrong-address adjudication processes are defined. These are not implementation details — they are product definition gaps."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": [
    { "violation": "Cannot define acceptance criteria when the fundamental product promise (guarantee vs best-effort) is contradicted between PM and Legal", "business_rule_broken": "The 2-hour delivery window's contractual nature is undefined — PM says guarantee, Legal says best-effort. Every downstream rule (refund triggers, customer-facing language, dispute resolution) depends on resolving this." }
  ]
}
```
